metadata.name: Bushido 1 — Devoted Retainer blocks, gets +1/+1 until end of turn
metadata.description: p1 controls Monastery Swiftspear (1/2, Haste, Prowess) and attacks p2. p2 controls Devoted Retainer (1/1, Bushido 1). p2 declares Devoted Retainer as a blocker. CR 702.45a: Bushido triggers during the declare blockers step — 'Whenever this creature blocks or becomes blocked, it gets +1/+1 until end of turn.' The trigger goes on the stack; both players pass priority; the trigger resolves, giving Devoted Retainer +1/+1 until end of turn, making it 2/2. In the combat damage step both creatures deal simultaneous damage (CR 510.1): Monastery Swiftspear (power 1) deals 1 damage to Devoted Retainer (now 2/2 from Bushido); Devoted Retainer (power 2 after Bushido) deals 2 damage to Monastery Swiftspear (toughness 2). SBA 704.5g: Monastery Swiftspear received 2 damage against 2 toughness — lethal — destroyed. Devoted Retainer received only 1 damage against 2 toughness (after Bushido) — survives. Without Bushido, Devoted Retainer (1/1) would have received lethal damage and died; Bushido 1 is what saves it.
metadata.generation_notes: Devoted Retainer oracle text (MCP verified): Creature — Human Samurai, {W}, 1/1, Bushido 1. CR 702.45a: 'Bushido N means: Whenever this creature blocks or becomes blocked, it gets +N/+N until end of turn.' The trigger fires during the declare blockers step when Devoted Retainer becomes blocked. Monastery Swiftspear oracle text (Available Cards list): 1/2, Haste, Prowess. Prowess only triggers on non-creature spells being cast — no spells are cast during this combat, so Prowess does not fire. Haste means Monastery Swiftspear can attack the turn it enters the battlefield (CR 702.10a); turn 1 is used for simplicity. At declare blockers: Devoted Retainer blocks Monastery Swiftspear; CR 509.1h makes Swiftspear a blocked creature; CR 702.45a fires the Bushido trigger (Devoted Retainer 'becomes blocked'). Trigger resolves: Devoted Retainer gets +1/+1 until end of turn, becoming 2/2. Combat damage step (CR 510.1): Monastery Swiftspear (power 1) assigns 1 damage to Devoted Retainer; Devoted Retainer (power 2 after Bushido boost) assigns 2 damage to Monastery Swiftspear. Damage is simultaneous (CR 510.2). SBA 704.5g: Monastery Swiftspear has toughness 2 and received 2 damage (2 >= 2 = lethal) — destroyed, moves to p1 graveyard. Devoted Retainer has toughness 2 (1 base + 1 Bushido) and received 1 damage (1 < 2 = not lethal) — survives. This scenario deliberately highlights that Bushido 1 saved Devoted Retainer: without the +1/+1, Devoted Retainer would be 1/1 and 1 damage from Swiftspear would be lethal (1 >= 1), destroying both creatures. With Bushido, only Swiftspear dies. p2 player takes no damage (attacker was fully blocked). The Bushido +1/+1 expires at end of turn (CR 514.2 cleanup). Two-player game sufficient — no multiplayer interactions required.

script.step_note: p1 declares Monastery Swiftspear as an attacker targeting p2. CR 508.1f: Swiftspear becomes tapped. Bushido does NOT trigger at declare attackers — CR 702.45a specifies it triggers when the creature 'blocks or becomes blocked,' which happens during the declare blockers step.

actions.description: Initial: Monastery Swiftspear untapped on p1 battlefield; Devoted Retainer untapped on p2 battlefield; life totals 40/40; stack empty
actions.note: Monastery Swiftspear has Haste (CR 702.10a) — it can attack on turn 1. Devoted Retainer is a 1/1 Samurai with Bushido 1. No spells are cast during combat so Prowess on Swiftspear will not trigger.

CR 508.1: p1 declares Monastery Swiftspear as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 508.1f: Monastery Swiftspear becomes tapped. CR 702.10a: Haste allows Swiftspear to attack on turn 1. Bushido does NOT trigger here — it triggers when the creature 'becomes blocked,' not when it attacks.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Monastery Swiftspear tapped (CR 508.1f), Devoted Retainer untapped, stack empty
actions.note: Swiftspear is now an attacking creature (tapped). No triggers have fired yet. Bushido triggers at the declare blockers step, not here.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to DeclareBlockers step.

script.step_note: p2 declares Devoted Retainer as a blocker for Monastery Swiftspear. CR 702.45a: Bushido triggers — Devoted Retainer 'becomes blocked' — one triggered ability is placed on the stack giving Devoted Retainer +1/+1 until end of turn.

actions.note: CR 509.1: p2 declares Devoted Retainer as a blocker for Monastery Swiftspear. CR 509.1a: Devoted Retainer is untapped — legal blocker. CR 509.1g: Devoted Retainer becomes a blocking creature. CR 509.1h: Monastery Swiftspear becomes a blocked creature. CR 702.45a: Devoted Retainer has Bushido 1; it just 'became blocked' — the Bushido triggered ability fires, placing one trigger on the stack: 'Devoted Retainer gets +1/+1 until end of turn.'

actions.description: After declare_blockers: Bushido trigger on the stack (count 1), both creatures still on battlefield
actions.note: CR 702.45a: The Bushido 1 triggered ability is on the stack. When it resolves, Devoted Retainer will get +1/+1 until end of turn, temporarily becoming a 2/2.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — the Bushido trigger (top of stack) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.45a: Bushido 1 trigger resolves — Devoted Retainer gets +1/+1 until end of turn. Devoted Retainer is now 2/2 (was 1/1).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Bushido triggered ability resolves. Devoted Retainer receives +1/+1 until end of turn, becoming 2/2.

actions.description: After Bushido trigger resolves: stack empty, both creatures still on battlefield, Devoted Retainer now effectively 2/2
actions.note: Bushido trigger has resolved. Devoted Retainer has a +1/+1 until-end-of-turn continuous effect applied, making it 2/2. Both creatures remain on the battlefield entering the combat damage step.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to CombatDamage step.

script.step_note: Combat damage (CR 510.1): Monastery Swiftspear (power 1, blocked) assigns 1 damage to Devoted Retainer (now 2/2 from Bushido); Devoted Retainer (power 2 after Bushido) assigns 2 damage to Monastery Swiftspear (toughness 2). Damage is simultaneous (CR 510.2). SBA 704.5g: Monastery Swiftspear has 2 damage marked against toughness 2 — lethal — destroyed. Devoted Retainer has 1 damage marked against toughness 2 (after Bushido) — not lethal — survives.

results.description: Monastery Swiftspear has toughness 2 and received 2 damage (from Devoted Retainer's power 2 after Bushido) — 2 >= 2 — lethal damage — destroyed

actions.note: CR 704.5g: Monastery Swiftspear has toughness 2 and 2 damage marked — lethal — destroyed and moves to p1's graveyard. Devoted Retainer has toughness 2 (1 base + 1 Bushido) and only 1 damage marked (from Swiftspear's power 1) — not lethal — Devoted Retainer survives. Without the Bushido +1/+1 bonus, Devoted Retainer would have been 1/1 and 1 damage from Swiftspear would be lethal (1 >= 1 toughness), killing it too. CR 704.3: SBAs are checked as a batch.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after combat damage and SBAs. Stack is empty — advance to EndOfCombat step.

actions.description: After combat damage and SBAs: p2 at 40 life (Swiftspear was blocked), Monastery Swiftspear in p1 graveyard, Devoted Retainer still on p2 battlefield
actions.note: CR 510.1c: Monastery Swiftspear was blocked — it assigned all 1 damage to Devoted Retainer, not to p2. p2 life remains 40. Devoted Retainer's toughness was boosted to 2 by Bushido 1; it received only 1 damage (Swiftspear's base power 1) — not lethal — Devoted Retainer survives on p2's battlefield. Monastery Swiftspear received 2 damage (Devoted Retainer's power 2 after Bushido) against its 2 toughness — lethal (SBA 704.5g) — destroyed, in p1's graveyard. The +1/+1 Bushido bonus on Devoted Retainer expires at the cleanup step (CR 514.2), returning it to 1/1.
